title: Do Corruption-Family Text Residuals Help Zero-Shot CLIP? A Controlled Baseline Study
description: This project proposes a controlled baseline study for corruption robustness in frozen zero-shot CLIP. It tests whether paired corruption-family text residuals contain family-specific signal beyond clean prompt ensembling, naive corruption prompts, a generic degraded-image residual, and random family reassignment.
motivation: The nearby literature already contains stronger prompt-side and unlabeled adaptation methods such as AutoCLIP, Frolic, BATCLIP, BendVLM, and PRISM, so the realistic gap is not a broad new method but a careful baseline analysis. A useful contribution under an 8-hour single-GPU budget is to determine whether explicit corruption-family language carries real family-specific information or mostly collapses into generic image-quality cues.
proposed_approach: Use one frozen CLIP backbone and restrict evaluation to gaussian noise, motion blur, fog, and JPEG compression on CIFAR-10-C and CIFAR-100-C. Construct shared family residuals from paired clean-versus-corrupted class prompts, estimate a lightweight family posterior from generic corruption prompts, and apply a small additive calibration to clean class prototypes. Make the study analysis-first: require a pilot gate, compare against clean prompt ensembling, naive corruption prompts, a generic low-quality residual, and random family reassignment, and keep projection as an optional diagnostic only if it improves family separation in the pilot. AutoCLIP is single-seed and optional only if the pilot is strongly positive and time remains.

related_work.title: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
related_work.authors: Alec Radford, Jong Wook Kim, Chris Hallacy, Aditya Ramesh, Gabriel Goh, Sandhini Agarwal, Girish Sastry, Amanda Askell, Pamela Mishkin, Jack Clark, Gretchen Krueger, Ilya Sutskever
related_work.how_it_differs: Provides the CLIP backbone and zero-shot prompting interface but does not study corruption-family text residuals.

related_work.title: Visual Classification via Description from Large Language Models
related_work.how_it_differs: Improves zero-shot classification through richer class descriptions, whereas this proposal studies shared corruption-family nuisance directions.

related_work.title: Test-Time Prompt Tuning for Zero-Shot Generalization in Vision-Language Models
related_work.authors: Manli Shu, Weili Nie, De-An Huang, Zhiding Yu, Tom Goldstein, Anima Anandkumar, Chaowei Xiao
related_work.how_it_differs: Optimizes prompts at test time, while the proposed study stays fully frozen and optimization-free.

related_work.title: AutoCLIP: Auto-tuning Zero-Shot Classifiers for Vision-Language Models
related_work.authors: Jan Hendrik Metzen, Piyapat Saranrittichai, Chaithanya Kumar Mummadi
related_work.how_it_differs: AutoCLIP is the closest executable prior because it improves frozen zero-shot classifiers from unlabeled data. This proposal is narrower and asks whether an explicit four-family residual bank has family-specific value beyond generic prompt strengthening.

related_work.title: Enhancing Zero-Shot Vision Models by Label-Free Prompt Distribution Learning and Bias Correcting
related_work.authors: Xingyu Zhu, Beier Zhu, Yi Tan, Shuo Wang, Yanbin Hao, Hanwang Zhang
related_work.how_it_differs: Frolic learns prompt distributions and bias correction broadly, while this proposal uses named corruption families and explicit controls to test family specificity.

related_work.title: Enhancing Robustness of CLIP to Common Corruptions through Bimodal Test-Time Adaptation
related_work.authors: Sarthak Kumar Maharana, Baoming Zhang, Leonid Karlinsky, Rogerio Feris, Yunhui Guo
related_work.how_it_differs: BATCLIP performs much heavier bimodal adaptation for corruption robustness, while this work targets a cheap frozen baseline and analysis.

related_work.title: BendVLM: Test-Time Debiasing of Vision-Language Embeddings
related_work.authors: Walter Gerych, Haoran Zhang, Kimia Hamidieh, Eileen Pan, Maanas Sharma, Thomas Hartvigsen, Marzyeh Ghassemi
related_work.how_it_differs: BendVLM studies more general embedding debiasing, which is why this proposal treats projection only as an optional diagnostic and not as a core novelty claim.

related_work.title: PRISM: Reducing Spurious Implicit Biases in Vision-Language Models with LLM-Guided Embedding Projection
related_work.authors: Mahdiyar Molahasani, Azadeh Motamedi, Michael Greenspan, Il-Min Kim, Ali Etemad
related_work.how_it_differs: PRISM uses LLM-guided embedding projection for general spurious-bias mitigation, while this proposal studies a hand-constructed corruption residual bank and asks whether it is family-specific at all.

related_work.title: Analysing the Robustness of Vision-Language-Models to Common Corruptions
related_work.authors: Muhammad Usama, Syeda Aisha Asim, Syed Bilal Ali, Syed Talal Wasim, Umair Bin Mansoor
related_work.how_it_differs: Characterizes corruption failures in VLMs but does not test prompt-side family-specific calibration.

related_work.title: Quality Text, Robust Vision: The Role of Language in Enhancing Visual Robustness of Vision-Language Models
related_work.authors: Futa Waseda, Saku Sugawara, Isao Echizen
related_work.how_it_differs: Uses language to improve robustness through training and tuning, whereas this proposal remains entirely in the frozen inference regime.

hypothesis: Paired corruption-family text residuals in CLIP space may carry usable family-specific signal, but only if they outperform both a generic degraded-image residual and random family reassignment. If that pilot criterion is met, a small additive frozen calibration rule should modestly improve four-family corruption robustness over zero-shot CLIP and naive corruption prompts; if not, the correct conclusion is that prompt-side corruption language mostly encodes generic degradation rather than family structure.

confirm: On the pilot, family residuals outperform both the generic degraded-image residual and random family reassignment on family identification or corrupted-image accuracy.
The proposed method improves mean corruption accuracy over zero-shot CLIP on both CIFAR-10-C and CIFAR-100-C four-family slices.
The method matches or exceeds clean prompt ensembling on at least one dataset and is not clearly worse on the other.
Clean accuracy drops by no more than 0.5 absolute points.
Observed gains are concentrated in matched corruption families rather than appearing as a uniform generic quality shift.
refute: Family residuals perform no better than a generic low-quality or degraded-image residual.
Random family reassignment performs similarly to correct family assignment.
The family posterior is driven mainly by semantics or generic image quality rather than corruption family.
Any gains vanish once compared against strong clean prompt ensembling or naive corruption prompts.
Projection provides no value, in which case it is removed from the final story rather than treated as a contribution.
